seo.title: AI Agent Drift Monitoring in Haystack Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent drift monitoring in Haystack agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: AI Agent Drift in Haystack pipelines manifests as subtle degradations in RAG performance, where retrievers return increasingly irrelevant documents, generators hallucinate outside retrieved context, and custom components fail silently without raising errors. These issues compound over time as models evolve or data distributions shift, leading to pipelines that appear functional but deliver poor user experiences. Haystack's composable nature makes drift hard to spot without systematic monitoring of component outputs and pipeline states.

howItManifests.symptom: Document scores drop below 0.7 threshold consistently, embedding drift detected in vector store
howItManifests.impact: Generator produces hallucinated answers, user trust erodes over weeks

howItManifests.symptom: Generated answers contain facts not present in top-k retrieved documents (>30% hallucination rate)
howItManifests.impact: Critical misinformation in production Q&A systems

howItManifests.symptom: Document list expected but receives single Document object, pipeline silently fails downstream
howItManifests.impact: Zero results returned to users without error logs

howItManifests.symptom: Custom reranker returns None instead of ranked documents, no exception raised
howItManifests.impact: Pipeline completes but returns empty results

detection.name: Retriever Relevance Drift Monitor
detection.description: Tracks average document score from retriever over time, alerts when drops >15% from baseline

detection.name: Generator Hallucination Detector
detection.description: Checks if generated answer contains information not present in retrieved context using keyword overlap
detection.code:
def detect_hallucination(generated_answer: str, context_docs: list) -> float:
    from sklearn.feature_extraction.text import TfidfVectorizer
    from sklearn.metrics.pairwise import cosine_similarity
    
    context_text = ' '.join([doc.content for doc in context_docs])
    
    vectorizer = TfidfVectorizer(stop_words='english', max_features=1000)
    tfidf_matrix = vectorizer.fit_transform([context_text, generated_answer])
    
    similarity = cosine_similarity(tfidf_matrix[0:1], tfidf_matrix[1:2])[0][0]
    hallucination_score = 1 - similarity
    
    if hallucination_score > 0.3:
        print(f'🚨 HALLUCINATION DETECTED: {hallucination_score:.2f}')
    
    return hallucination_score

detection.name: Pipeline Type Safety Checker
detection.description: Validates component output types match expected pipeline contracts

fixes.name: Dynamic Retriever Score Threshold
fixes.description: Automatically adjusts retrieval threshold based on 7-day rolling baseline
fixes.code:
from haystack.components.retrievers import InMemoryEmbeddingRetriever
import numpy as np

class AdaptiveRetriever(InMemoryEmbeddingRetriever):
    def __init__(self, **kwargs):
        super().__init__(**kwargs)
        self.score_history = []
        self.window_size = 50
    
    def run(self, query: str, top_k: int = 5, **kwargs):
        result = super().run(query=query, top_k=top_k*2, **kwargs)
        docs = result['documents']
        
        # Calculate adaptive threshold
        if len(self.score_history) >= self.window_size:
            threshold = np.percentile(self.score_history[-self.window_size:], 25)
        else:
            threshold = 0.5
        
        filtered_docs = [d for d in docs if (d.score or 0) >= threshold]
        result['documents'] = filtered_docs[:top_k]
        
        return result

fixes.name: Context-Grounded Generator
fixes.description: Forces generator to stay within retrieved context with explicit instructions

fixes.name: Error-Bubbling Custom Component
fixes.description: Ensures custom components raise descriptive errors instead of returning None

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Drift Monitoring Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates seamlessly as a custom Haystack component that automatically baselines your pipeline's healthy performance across all common drift vectors: retriever relevance, generator faithfulness, type safety, and component reliability. Once integrated, Vex continuously monitors production traffic, maintains rolling baselines of component outputs, and triggers automated alerts or rollbacks when drift exceeds configured thresholds. The platform's ML-powered anomaly detection adapts to your specific pipeline patterns, catching subtle degradations before they impact users.

Vex provides one-click remediation templates that inject fixes directly into your Haystack pipeline YAML, including adaptive thresholds, grounded prompts, and type-safe wrappers. Production teams using Vex report 80% faster drift detection and 60% reduction in manual debugging time for Haystack RAG pipelines. The integration requires zero changes to your existing pipeline logic.
vexAutomation.code:
from haystack import Pipeline
from vex_sdk.haystack import VexMonitor
from haystack_integrations.document_stores.in_memory import InMemoryDocumentStore

# Install: pip install haystack-ai vex-sdk

vex_monitor = VexMonitor(
    project_id="your-vex-project",
    pipeline_name="rag-pipeline",
    drift_thresholds={
        'retriever_score': 0.15,
        'hallucination_rate': 0.10,
        'type_errors': 0.05
    }
)

# Add Vex as first component in ANY Haystack pipeline
pipeline = Pipeline()
pipeline.add_component("vex_monitor", vex_monitor)

# Connect to your existing components
pipeline.connect("vex_monitor.output", "your_retriever.query")

# Vex auto-baselines and alerts on drift
pipeline.run({
    "vex_monitor": {"query": "user question"},
    "your_retriever": {"query": "user question"}
})
